column221:
I think coastal and any environmental issue should start with large corporations and their pollution.  Need better solutions especially with plastics and use of plastics.  Californias touts how we are liberal and environmental yet trash and waste is dumped everywhere especially in entertainment area like concert venues or fairs etc without much regard to recycling or non plastic use. 

I believe plastics are really killing the environment including the ocean and people.  From start to finish the plastics industry does not reveal what they use to make the plastics and how to properly dispose of the their waste in production nor ours as end user.   

We also need to change our concept of disposable products and food containment.  Too much plastic-just walk into a grocery store or costco.  

Anyway it’s not going to be solved in a day but i believe lots of education and media needs to get behind it-not as kip service but real action and prestige to save our environment and oceans

column221:
Growing up in Encinitas, California and then going to UCSD for undergrad I have a very large appreciation for marine life and the ocean. Seeing what extreme weather events (rain followed by extreme heat or droughts) have on coastal landscapes and marine life is alarming.

As a graduate student, I completed a social dimensions of climate change course and it really highlighted how climate change affects everyone including marine life/coasts.

We need to take steps to protect our oceans and marine life. Rising sea levels, ocean acidification, depletion or carbon reservoirs are among some of the concerns I have.  In addition to extreme weather events.

column221: I believe the true power of the positive ions emitted by the oceans impact on the human, mental and physical, emotional and spiritual well-being has yet to be truly discovered. I find it calming and soothing to sit by the Sea. I breathe in as the way builds and out as the wave crashes as a form of therapy for relaxation and meditation. I appreciate any sanctuaries that support protecting marine life around the world and especially in California. My home state or I was born and raised on the coast of California and spent most of my life here. I did leave for 20 years and returned. It's absolutely beautiful. Water is over 75% of the planet and the human body. I love to go to the beach and I need to go more often. I travel by train. It takes almost an hour. I'm in a wheelchair. Access is difficult. I appreciate my opportunity to participate in your survey and your efforts will be rewarded by the universe
